title: ExReg: Using Automated Example Generation to Guide Candidate Disambiguation and Validation in Regular Expression Construction
data:
H Muchtar, I Shevrin, S Maoz- ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and, 2026
Regular expressions (regexes) are widely used in software development but remain 
difficult to author and validate due to their compact syntax and subtle semantics. 
While large language models (LLMs) can now generate regexes from natural-
language descriptions, their outputs often miss developer intent, and existing 
refinement techniques rely on developers to craft positive and negative examples
especially discriminatory ones that expose fine-grained semantic differences

title: Large Language Models for Quantum Software Engineering: Opportunities and Challenges in Modeling, Generation, Testing, and Repair
data:
J Ye, Q Hu, F Zhang, S Xia, X Guo, X Wu, Y Lyu, J Zhao- ACM Transactions on, 2026
Large Language Models (LLMs) have already reshaped many aspects of traditional 
software engineering, from code generation and debugging to automated testing and 
maintenance. As an important research sub-domain in the software engineering 
area, quantum software engineering (QSE) is poised to undergo a similar 
transformation. As QSE moves toward broader adoption, including the generation of 
quantum code, program testing, and automated program repair, LLMs will likely play
